titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to Patrick Joseph Brady, 9 June 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926), assistant under-secretary for Ireland, to Patrick Joseph Brady (1868–1943) an Irish solicitor and politician. The letter refers to the detention of Otto Krautwurst (b.1878), a German professor held as an illegal alien. It states that he is currently held at Oldcastle Prisoner of War camp and that Messers O'keeffe and Lynch, solicitors, can represent the man on the grounds of ill health, however there is no intention on releasing him.Patrick Joseph Brady represented the Stephen’s Green division for the Irish Parliamentary Party in Westminster from 1910-1918. Professor Krautwurst was under surveillance since April 1910 by the DMP. He had committed no crime but had purchased “a substantial dwelling facing the sea,' a house in Sandymount at 17 Strand Road.

msIdentifier.collection: National Archive of Ireland-Chief Secretary's Office-Registered Papers, NAI/CSO/RP/1916/5612/9487/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
9487 9th June 1916. Sir, In reply to your letter of the 3rd instant
regarding the case of Mr. Otto Krautwurst, at
present under detention in Pri soners of War
Camp at Oldcastle, I am directed by the Lords
Justices to inform you that if Messrs O'Keeffe
& Lynch make any representations on the grounds
of ill-health they will be considered, but there
is no present intention of releasing this man. I am
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, (sd)E. O'Farrell Patrick J. Brady, Esq., M.P. Glena, Booterstown, Co. Dublin.
